output: Batch 2 completed as a read-only audit/inventory pass. I recorded the T2/T3 findings and SC2/SC3 acknowledgments in `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1c-from-docs-state-20260416-2053/execution_batch_2.json`. Key outcomes: `VideoEditorProvider` owns additional load-bearing orchestration beyond the three contexts and those pathways are now explicitly classified; `useAddToVideoEditor`'s nullable mounted-vs-staged contract is confirmed as a hard migration boundary; the live consumer/test inventory for the timeline contexts is refreshed; the hot-path refs are classified into keep vs removable context-packaging shims; and the `TimelineCanvas` -> `TrackListRenderer` -> `SortableRow` prop fan-out is confirmed as the render-isolation risk that must be hand-finished later.

task_updates.executor_notes: Audit completed from the live provider and external nullable consumer. `VideoEditorProvider.tsx` still owns more than the three timeline contexts: (1) `DataProviderWrapper` / runtime boundary: keep provider-local; not store state. (2) effects bootstrap via `useEffects`, `useEffectResources`, and `useEffectRegistry`: keep provider-local orchestration, or at most extract to a sibling bootstrap hook; not store-owned. (3) `AgentChatBridgeRegistration`: extract/keep as a sibling bridge component that reads store selectors, not store state itself; its `opsRef` and `clipsRef` are load-bearing stale-closure guards to avoid register/unregister churn. (4) pending-add drain for `ADD_GENERATION_QUERY_PARAM` plus staged localStorage queue (`pendingAddGenerationId`, `consumedAddGenerationRef`, `drainedStagedRef`, `drainInFlightRef`, async loader loop, query-param cleanup): extract to a sibling hook or keep provider-local, but do not move into the store because it is router/localStorage side-effect orchestration; preserve mounted-vs-staged semantics. `editorRef.current` inside this flow is load-bearing because the async loop registers and drops multiple generations over time and must always read the latest editor surface. (5) lightbox shell state `lightboxAssetKey`, `lightboxClipId`, `lightboxQuery`, `lightboxFallbackMedia`, close handler, and missing-data cleanup effect: keep provider-local UI/query state, or extract to a sibling lightbox hook; not store-owned timeline state. (6) `onDoubleClickAsset`: keep provider-local or move with the lightbox sibling hook because it only opens provider-owned lightbox state. (7) `navResult` from `useVideoEditorLightboxNavigation`: already an extracted sibling hook; keep outside the store. (8) `buildVideoEditorLightboxMedia` helper: pure helper, not store state. (9) context assembly (`editorData`, `editorOps`, chrome/playback providers) is the part that should migrate to the new timeline store/bootstrap surface. External nullable-safe contract audit: `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useAddToVideoEditor.ts` currently branches on `useTimelineEditorOpsSafe()` / `useTimelineEditorDataSafe()` returning `null`; mounted editor means immediate register+drop, unmounted editor means first click stages in localStorage and second click navigates. This contract cannot be replaced with a global always-present default store. The migration needs an explicit availability boundary, e.g. a mounted timeline-store provider plus nullable-safe selector hooks, so callers can still distinguish `editor mounted` from `no editor mounted`.

Fresh grep-backed migration inventory completed. Non-test app consumers of the three timeline contexts / safe hooks are: `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useAddToVideoEditor.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/CompactPreview.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/AssetPanel.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/PreviewPanel.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx` itself. Test surface that must be migrated includes direct hook consumers in `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` plus context-module mocks in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx`. Ref classification by live callsite: (1) `VideoEditorProvider.tsx` `editorRef.current`: keep, load-bearing async pending-add stale-closure protection. (2) `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` exposing `dataRef`, `selectedClipIdsRef`, `additiveSelectionRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, and `interactionStateRef` through one large memoized context object is mostly context-identity/stability plumbing; remove that packaging layer once store selectors/adapters exist, but preserve the underlying refs where hot-path hooks still need them. (3) `TimelineCanvas.tsx` reading `dataRef` only to feed `useClipResizeGesture`: keep via a store-backed mutable adapter; this is load-bearing for live resize sessions. (4) `useClipDrag.ts` `dataRef`, `selectedClipIdsRef`, `additiveSelectionRef`, and `interactionStateRef`: keep, all are load-bearing for pointer-sequence correctness, drag threshold transitions, multi-select preservation, and gesture-end notifications. (5) `TimelineEditor.tsx` `interactionStateRef` gate passed to pinned-group sync and resize props: keep, load-bearing interaction suppression. `selectedClipIdsRef` / `additiveSelectionRef` passed into `useClipDrag`: keep, load-bearing. `dataRef.current` reads inside duplicate/trim/expand/final-video and pinned-group helpers: keep as synchronized latest-data adapter, not a context-identity cope. (6) `VideoEditorShell.tsx` `editorData.dataRef.current` combined with `editorData.selectedClipIds` for keyboard delete is load-bearing synchronized-read behavior; migration needs one adapter or selector bundle that preserves same-operation freshness. (7) `useTimelinePersistence.ts` `dataRef` and `interactionStateRef`: keep, load-bearing async save/conflict lifecycle. (8) `usePollSync.ts` `dataRef`, `pendingOpsRef`, and `interactionStateRef`: keep, load-bearing poll gating / deferred replay. (9) `useExternalDrop.ts` `dataRef` and `pendingOpsRef`: keep, load-bearing upload/drop async bookkeeping. Row-prop fan-out risk confirmed: `TimelineCanvas.tsx` computes shared `rowResizePreview`, `resizeClampedActionId`, `selectedTrackId`, `getActionRender`, and multiple handlers centrally, then `TrackListRenderer.tsx` forwards them to every `SortableRow`. Because `SortableRow` also renders every action in its row, selector narrowing alone will not satisfy the render-isolation gate; the row/action prop surface itself has to be reduced so non-dragged rows do not receive changing shared props during drag/resize.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The provider-owned pathways were enumerated explicitly and each one was classified as store-owned only for the context assembly layer, provider-local for runtime/query/UI shell state, or extracted sibling-hook behavior for AgentChat, pending-add, and lightbox navigation. The external `useAddToVideoEditor` nullable-safe mounted-vs-staged contract was called out as a hard boundary to preserve.

Yes. A fresh grep-backed consumer/test inventory was captured, the hot-path refs were classified by live callsite into keep-vs-remove buckets, and the concrete `TimelineCanvas` -> `TrackListRenderer` -> `SortableRow` prop fan-out was identified as the render-isolation risk that selectors alone will not solve.
